response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience in software development, including full-stack roles, but their background is heavily skewed towards web technologies (JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, React) and lacks depth in the core C++ and industrial automation requirements of the role. While they demonstrate strong problem-solving and leadership skills, their recent work does not align closely with the technical competencies needed for this position.
strengths: Proven leadership and mentorship experience at top-tier tech companies (Google, Meta).
Strong background in event-driven architectures, microservices, and scalable systems (e.g., at Sociomantic, RIDE Capital).
Demonstrated ability to work across the stack and adapt to new technologies (e.g., DLang, Kubernetes, IoT-adjacent projects).
concerns: Limited recent hands-on experience with C++ (last noted in 2015 at National Instruments).
No explicit mention of industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443, or secure development lifecycle (SDL) practices.
Lack of direct experience with industrial automation protocols (e.g., Modbus, OPC UA) or cloud platforms like Azure.
